{{Quote|The TSF isn't cut out to handle all the problems on Citadel Station right now. We need Republic assistance.|TSF officer|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
The '''Telos Security Force''', often referred to as '''TSF''', managed [[Telos IV|Telos's]] [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|security]] before the [[Bombing of Telos IV|destruction]] of the planet's surface during the [[Jedi Civil War]].

==History==
[[File:Tsforce.jpg|thumb|left|210px|A TSF officer in full uniform]]
At the beginning of the [[Dark Wars]], the TSF, now headquartered on the newly built, [[Citadel Station]] was charged with [[Planetary Security Forces|protecting the efforts]] of the [[Telosian Restoration Project]] on Telos IV. However, it was often ineffective due to infiltration of the [[Telosian Council]]—the acting government over Citadel Station—by [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] officials, and the influence that corrupt organizations such as Czerka and the [[Exchange]] held over Citadel Station.

When the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]] arrived on Citadel Station aboard the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' in [[3951 BBY]], the TSF arrested her and her companions [[Atton Rand]] and [[Kreia]] for their supposed involvement in the destruction of the [[Peragus Mining Facility]] and failed to prevent her ship and [[Droid/Legends|droid]], [[T3-M4]], from being stolen by [[Brianna]], a [[Handmaiden Sisters|Handmaiden]] of [[Atris]]. Despite this inauspicious beginning, the Exile [[Skirmishes on Citadel Station|helped]] the TSF locate a missing man named [[Batono]], uncover the origins of a [[Batu Rem impostor|bounty hunter]] who had infiltrated the ranks of the TSF, find two criminals who had escaped from TSF headquarters, and stop a smuggling ring on the station led by [[Samhan Dobo]].

The TSF would later defend Citadel Station from [[Darth Nihilus]] during the [[Battle of Telos IV]], fighting alongside [[Canderous Ordo]]'s [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], [[Onderon Royalist]] soldiers from [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Talia (Onderon)|Talia]], [[Khoonda Militia]] soldiers from [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], as well as the Jedi Exile and her companions.

During the Exile's stay, members of the TSF included [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Dol Grenn]], [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Yima]], Officer [[Soka Linu]], and Officer [[Batu Rem]].

Thousands of years later, the TSF would participate in the [[Telosian Civil War]], fighting on the side of their government and the Telosian governor, [[Crion]] and his son, [[Xanatos]] against the Telosian population and the Jedi.

==Behind the scenes==
The TSF had a larger role in [[Obsidian Entertainment]]'s original vision of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Yima]] would give the player character—known as the Jedi Exile—a quest to expose the corruption of her fellow TSF lieutenant [[Atallo]], who was covering up the illegal Czerka weapons trade on [[Citadel Station]]. However, this quest was later cut, and neither Yima nor Atallo appear in the final version of the game—although Yima is mentioned by [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Dol Grenn]] as investigating the [[Batu Rem impostor]] incident.
